[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/65/%C4%90ur%C4%91evac_street.jpg/250px-%C4%90ur%C4%91evac_street.jpg)](/wiki/File:%C4%90ur%C4%91evac_street.jpg)

A street in Đurđevac

**Đurđevac** is a town in the [Koprivnica-Križevci County](/wiki/Koprivnica-Kri%C5%BEevci_County "Koprivnica-Križevci County") in [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Until 1918, Đurđevac (named *Militär Sanct Georgen* before 1850[[3]](#cite_note-3)) was part of the [Austrian monarchy](/wiki/Austrian_monarchy "Austrian monarchy") ([Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Croatia-Slavonia "Kingdom of Croatia-Slavonia") after the [compromise of 1867](/wiki/Compromise_of_1867 "Compromise of 1867")), in the [Croatian Military Frontier](/wiki/Croatian_Military_Frontier "Croatian Military Frontier"), under the WARASDIN-ST. GEORGENER Regiment N°VI.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the late 19th century and early 20th century, Đurđevac was a district capital in the [Bjelovar-Križevci County](/wiki/Bjelovar-Kri%C5%BEevci_County "Bjelovar-Križevci County") of the Kingdom.

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Climate")]

Since records began in 1960, the highest temperature recorded at the local weather station was 38.6 °C (101.5 °F), on 10 August 2017.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The coldest temperature was −28.3 °C (−18.9 °F), on 16 January 1963.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

Further information on the: [Molve gas project](/wiki/Molve_gas_project "Molve gas project")

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

According to the 2021 census, there are a total of 7,378 inhabitants in the municipality, in the following settlements:[[2]](#cite_note-Census_2021-2)

* [Budrovac](/w/index.php?title=Budrovac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Budrovac (page does not exist)"), population 277
* [Čepelovac](/w/index.php?title=%C4%8Cepelovac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Čepelovac (page does not exist)"), population 302
* **Đurđevac**, population 5,834
* [Grkine](/wiki/Grkine "Grkine"), population 121
* [Mičetinac](/w/index.php?title=Mi%C4%8Detinac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mičetinac (page does not exist)"), population 146
* [Severovci](/w/index.php?title=Severovci&action=edit&redlink=1 "Severovci (page does not exist)"), population 92
* [Sirova Katalena](/w/index.php?title=Sirova_Katalena&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sirova Katalena (page does not exist)"), population 226
* [Suha Katalena](/wiki/Suha_Katalena "Suha Katalena"), population 285
* [Sveta Ana](/w/index.php?title=Sveta_Ana,_Croatia&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sveta Ana, Croatia (page does not exist)"), population 95

In the census of 2021, [Croats](/wiki/Croats "Croats") formed an absolute majority at 92.55%.[[2]](#cite_note-Census_2021-2)

The citizens of the town colloquially call themselves "Picoki".

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Administration")]

The current mayor of Đurđevac is Hrvoje Janči and the Đurđevac Town Council consists of 13 seats.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Groups | Councilors per group |
| [Independents](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") | 11 / 13 |
| [SDP](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_of_Croatia "Social Democratic Party of Croatia") | 2 / 13 |
| Source:[[7]](#cite_note-7) | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

## Sights and events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Sights and events")]

* [Old town Đurđevac](/wiki/Old_town_%C4%90ur%C4%91evac "Old town Đurđevac")
* [Picokijada](/wiki/Picokijada "Picokijada") - Legenda o picokima
* [Church of st. George in Đurđevac](/w/index.php?title=Church_of_st._George_in_%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Church of st. George in Đurđevac (page does not exist)")
* [Sands of Đurđevac](/wiki/Sands_of_%C4%90ur%C4%91evac "Sands of Đurđevac")
* [Park-Forest Đurđevac](/w/index.php?title=Park-Forest_%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Park-Forest Đurđevac (page does not exist)")
* [Seperacija](/wiki/Seperacija "Seperacija"), an artificial lake
* [Čivićevac](/wiki/%C4%8Civi%C4%87evac "Čivićevac"), the perennial river through the town

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C4%90ur%C4%91evac&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Boris Braun](/wiki/Boris_Braun "Boris Braun") - member of the notable Braun family, University professor and honorary Citizen of Đurđevac
* [Mladen Markač](/wiki/Mladen_Marka%C4%8D "Mladen Markač") - Commander of Croatian Special Police during [Operation Storm](/wiki/Operation_Storm "Operation Storm") during the [Croatian War of Independence](/wiki/Croatian_War_of_Independence "Croatian War of Independence") (1991–1995), and afterwards held the rank of [Colonel General](/wiki/Colonel_General "Colonel General")

## Twin city

* [![Hungary](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Flag_of_Hungary.svg/40px-Flag_of_Hungary.svg.png)](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") [Gödöllő](/wiki/G%C3%B6d%C3%B6ll%C5%91 "Gödöllő"), Hungary
